title: Wilcoxia viperina
intro: Wilcoxia viperina, also known as Wilcox's vipergrass, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the family Apiaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Wilcoxia viperina include sesquiterpene lactones, phenolic acids, and coumarins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Wilcoxia viperina is commonly used in preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures to help alleviate symptoms of bronchitis, asthma, and allergies.

The family Commelinaceae is further divided into several orders, with Wilcoxia viperina belonging to the order Commelinales. Within the order Commelinales, it is classified as a member of the family Commelinaceae.
